title: COBOL Compile & Execute on MVS
description: Create a COBOL program, write JCL, submit the compile-link-go job. Trace the deferred execution model.

steps.title: Create a COBOL Source Member
steps.screen:
ISPF Edit — HERC01.COBOL(HELLO)

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. HELLO.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       DATA DIVISION.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
           DISPLAY 'HELLO FROM MVS'.
           STOP RUN.

Command ===>
steps.expected: Write a minimal COBOL program in a PDS member.
steps.hint: COBOL on MVS uses fixed-format columns: 7-72 for code, 73-80 for sequence numbers.

steps.title: Write Compile JCL
steps.expected: JCL to compile and link-edit the COBOL program.
steps.hint: IGYWCL is the compile-link procedure. SYSLMOD is the output load module library.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor
steps.screen:
SDSF STATUS DISPLAY

NJobname  JobID    Owner  Prty  Status
-COBCOMP  JOB00201 HERC01 1     ACTIVE

Step COBOL executing...
Step LKED executing...

IEWL530I END OF LINKEDIT
IEF142I COBCOMP COBOL  - STEP WAS EXECUTED - COND CODE 0000
IEF142I COBCOMP LKED   - STEP WAS EXECUTED - COND CODE 0000
steps.expected: Both compile and link steps complete with condition code 0000.
steps.hint: Condition code 0000 = success. Code 4 = warning. Code 8+ = error.

steps.title: Write Execute JCL
steps.expected: JCL to execute the compiled COBOL load module.
steps.hint: STEPLIB points to the load library containing the HELLO program.

steps.title: View Output
steps.screen:
SDSF OUTPUT — COBRUN JOB00202

1                                       JOB00202
0         HELLO FROM MVS

IEF142I COBRUN STEP1 - STEP WAS EXECUTED - COND CODE 0000
IEF285I  HERC01.LOAD                      KEPT
IEF373I STEP/STEP1/START 2026060.2115
IEF374I STEP/STEP1/STOP  2026060.2115 CPU 0MIN 00.01SEC
steps.expected: Program output 'HELLO FROM MVS' appears in SYSOUT.
steps.hint: The output goes to the JES spool, not a terminal — this is deferred execution.

steps.title: Deferred Execution Model
steps.screen:
COBOL Execution Model on MVS

Write source -> Compile (IGYCRCTL) -> Link (IEWL) -> Execute

Each step is a SEPARATE job or step.
Execution is DEFERRED — not interactive.
Identity is bound at JOB card submission time.

Security implication:
A malicious COBOL program submitted under a privileged
user's identity runs with THEIR RACF authority.
steps.expected: Understand the compile-link-go pipeline and its security implications.
steps.hint: The gap between submission and execution is the mainframe's unique attack surface.

steps.title: Lab Complete
steps.screen:
COBOL Basics Lab Complete

You have:
- Written COBOL source in a PDS member
- Compiled with IGYWCL procedure
- Link-edited to a load module
- Executed via JCL batch job
- Observed deferred execution model

Key insight: All execution on MVS is fundamentally
batch-oriented, even interactive TSO sessions.
steps.expected: Complete the full COBOL compile-link-go cycle on MVS.
steps.hint: Understanding this pipeline is essential for analyzing JCL-based attack vectors.
